rows.notes: Consensus reached on description and type. Both extractors identified this as a clear and legible page title with no monetary amounts. Vertical ruling lines are clear.

rows.description: Pd mr Coperats Decr: at xmas 1731
rows.notes: Description from `gemini-flash` ('Coperats', 'xmas') is more accurate. For pence, `gemini-flash` interprets the small mark after '8' as 0.5 (half-penny), which is a plausible historical notation for a small loop/flourish. `gpt-5-mini` considered it a flourish without monetary value. Given consistency with later half-penny notations in the ledger, `gemini-flash`'s interpretation is preferred.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Coperats made at Christmas (December) 1731.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Coperats at Christmas 1731, amounting to £43 18s 8½d.

rows.notes: `gemini-flash`'s description ('Socijs') is more accurate to the archaic Latin spelling in the image, showing a long 'j'.

`gemini-flash` provides a more complete and accurate description ('Fest. Nab: {Stip: &c'). The 'd' symbol after '8' pence clearly indicates 0.5 (half-penny), as correctly identified by `gemini-flash`. `gpt-5-mini` missed the fraction and had a less complete description.
rows.english_description: Stipend and related allowances to the Rector and Fellows for the Nativity (Christmas) feast.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend and related allowances to the Rector and Fellows for the Nativity feast, amounting to £18 6s 8½d.

rows.english_description: Third part of an augmentation (designation 'Cod' unclear in manuscript).

rows.notes: This row was correctly identified as a distinct group header by `gpt-5-mini` (as row 10 'Ad Test: Mic:'), which `gemini-flash` omitted as a separate row. Visually confirmed as 'Ad Fest: Mic:' functioning as a header for the subsequent grouped entries. Corrected `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Test' to 'Fest' based on visual verification and set `row_type` to 'header'.

Consensus on whole amounts. `gemini-flash`'s description ('olag:', 'Witenham') is more accurate. It also correctly identifies the 'd' symbol after '6' pence as 0.5 (half-penny), which `gpt-5-mini` missed.
rows.english_description: Payment relating to Long Wittenham, likely a church oblation or church-related disbursement at Michaelmas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment relating to Long Wittenham at Michaelmas, amounting to £17 10s 6½d.

rows.notes: `gpt-5-mini` correctly identified this as a separate entry with amounts. `gemini-flash` incorrectly merged it with a preceding header. Corrected `gpt-5-mini`'s description from 'Rest:' to 'Rect:' based on visual verification. Amounts are clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Rector and Fellows.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector and Fellows, amounting to £24 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Visually, the pence column for this row clearly shows the annotation 'Ad', which is not a numerical value. `gemini-flash` correctly omits a pence digit and treats 'Ad' as extraneous. `gpt-5-mini`'s reading of '4' pence is incorrect. Setting pence to 0 as there is no numerical digit.

Consensus on description and whole amounts. `gemini-flash` correctly identifies the 'q' symbol after '6' pence as 0.25 (a farthing). `gpt-5-mini`'s interpretation as 0.75 (3q) is not supported by the visual evidence of a clear single 'q'.

rows.description: Sic introduct: rest: a Dno computante
rows.notes: Consensus on whole amounts. `gemini-flash`'s description ('rest:') is visually more accurate than `gpt-5-mini`'s ('vert:'). The 'q' symbol after '10' pence clearly indicates 0.25 (a farthing), as correctly identified by `gemini-flash`. `gpt-5-mini` interpreted it as a flourish.

`gemini-flash`'s spelling of 'Conybeare' is visually accurate. Both assign no amounts. Keeping `gemini-flash`'s `row_type` as 'entry' as these are specific records of individuals rather than section titles, for consistency with other name entries.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of Joannes Conybeare, Rector (no amount recorded).
